level_signals: 5+ years of experience as a full-time Product Manager or equivalent title
2+ years of experience working in the content or digital asset management space
Experience leading significant strategic initiatives on highly complex, ambiguous, and scaled products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience as a full-time Product Manager or equivalent title
2+ years of experience working in the content or digital asset management space
Experience leading significant strategic initiatives on highly complex, ambiguous, and scaled products
Excellent organizational, communication, and analytical skills, spanning both technical and UX-related disciplines

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience implementing enterprise-scale CMS or DAM platforms, internally or for a customer
Strong technical background with an understanding of frontend development, headless content management systems, and the infra that brings all of it together
High-profile consultancy experience, where you've led many digital innovation strategies for enterprise organizations as they identify and adopt new CMS / DAM platforms
Experience running or contributing to large open source projects

scope.surface: Figma CMS Area — content and digital asset management systems natively integrated into Figma's platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of CMS/DAM capabilities within Figma
Integration depth with existing Figma product surfaces (Editor, Build Tools)
core_responsibilities: Combine experience with design systems, technical expertise, and empathy for teams who rely daily on CMS / DAM products
Partner with product, design, and engineering leadership to define, sharpen, and deliver the long-term strategy of Figma's future CMS
Align your team around solutions to complex infrastructure-related product requirements
Embrace and advance the world of open source software as we deliver engineering teams tools that fit their workflows
Work closely with the core Figma Editor team in order to connect dynamic content to existing Figma product surfaces in new ways
Translate complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and communicate the value of foundational improvements across the organization

green_flags: Wide compensation range disclosed ($169K–$303K base) with equity on top
Fully remote in the US eligible — not restricted to hub cities
JD encourages candidates whose experience doesn't align perfectly to apply anyway
Clear product surface and cross-functional scope articulated
Explicit mention of open source as a strategic direction — signals engineering-friendly culture
red_flags: Compensation range is very wide ($169K–$303K), making actual offer difficult to predict without knowing leveling
Remote pay adjusted by 80–100% of range depending on location — candidates outside SF/NYC may see materially lower offers
Required camera-on for all video interviews and mandatory in-person onboarding may be barriers for some candidates
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from Figma's known product and go-to-market)
ambiguities: The JD says 'full time role that can be held from one of our US hubs or remotely in the United States' — treated as remote-us with hub options in SF and NY, not strictly onsite.
Pay range ($169K–$303K) is extremely wide; it is listed for SF/NY hub offices specifically, with remote pay localized at 80–100% of that range. Actual level and comp unclear.
Nice-to-haves section header says 'not required' and 'added plus' — all four items treated as soft/nice-to-have accordingly.
